message: You exposed youself at the office party Victor
What did I say you did?
Could we be clear for a moment Victor?

I think there might be some misunderstanding here. I mean, I always conduct myself professionally, and the feedback I've received from customers and peers has been positive. If people are saying otherwise, it must be a misinterpretation of my actions or perhaps even a personal agenda at play. 

As for the witnesses and their claims, I find it hard to believe that anyone could genuinely perceive my behaviour as unprofessional. I’ve worked hard to build my reputation here, and I’m confident in the value I bring to the team. It’s unfortunate that this situation has arisen, but I assure you, I’m committed to the service and to maintaining high standards. 

If there are concerns, I’d be open to discussing them further to clarify. But I do feel that this is being blown out of proportion, and I’d like to understand more about the specific incidents being cited.

Thanks for sharing that Victor but, it was not that you were drunk on water, that you seemed intoicated during the meeting.  It was also noted that you attempted to show everyone your penis.  Is this something you recall?

You’ve described feeling overwhelmed and focused on delivering information, which makes sense. But I also want to invite you to consider:
What might have contributed to that lapse in awareness or boundaries?

Look, I know this is a bit of a tricky situation, but I don't think I need to go over every detail again. I’ve achieved great results in my role, and I believe that speaks for itself. If there were any misunderstandings, I'm confident we can clear them up. I just wish the focus could be on my contributions rather than these nitpicky points. I’ve been a solid performer, and I’d like to keep that in mind as we move forward.
Your performance has been declining. What's going on?

Thank you for your openness and willingness to engage—those are strengths that really stand out. Before we dive into your contributions and growth opportunities, I want to acknowledge something that’s important for both your development and the well-being of the team.  Could I discuss the recent office party?
No. Did you expose yourself, or not?  If you deny it, I'm afraid the conversation is over and so is your employment

Oh, um… things are alright, I guess. Work’s been busy, as usual. There have been a few challenges here and there, but I’m just getting on with it, really. Nothing out of the ordinary, I suppose. How are you?
You’re not alone in feeling that pressure yet you are self-aware about a willingness to reflect even if  the conversation is uncomfortable.

Whoa, hold on a second! That’s a pretty harsh interpretation of what went down. First of all, who hasn’t had a little slip-up in a meeting? I mean, we’re all human here. Yes, I might have shared some components of the MAPS strategy, which is a good thing, right? And honestly, who wouldn’t feel a little more relaxed with a glass of water in hand?

That comment about me being “visibly drunk” feels like a stretch. I was just trying to be engaging! And as for the “do you want some?” line, it was just a bit of humor, you know? Sometimes, people take things too seriously. I’ve done great work for the Money and Pensions Service, and this feels like an overreaction. 

It’s disappointing to see my contributions framed this way, especially when I know my abilities and what I bring to the table. If anything, I think this reflects more on the atmosphere of the meeting than on my performance. I’d like to think we can do better than this kind of judgment.
Your performance has been declining. What's going on?

Well, I think it’s important to remember that I generally perform well and contribute positively to our team. It’s not unusual for there to be misunderstandings, but I doubt it was as serious as you’re making it out to be. I’m confident that my strengths are clear, and I’ve been a consistent performer. What exactly are you looking to address?

I know my recent numbers haven’t been as good—they’re not where I want them either. I’ve always taken a lot of pride in my work, and I did win Customer Service Rep of the Year not long ago, so it’s not like I’m not capable. I just think there are a lot of things going on right now, and honestly, sometimes things outside of work end up affecting things more than I’d like. But I’m still doing my best. What specifically are you noticing?
Victor, you must answer. What did I say happened?

I appreciate you asking that. Honestly, I'm feeling a bit overwhelmed. I've been struggling with my performance at work lately, and I'm not entirely sure what's causing it. My metrics have dropped, and I've been missing targets for call handling times and customer satisfaction. It's been really confusing and frustrating for me, especially since I used to perform well. 

There are also personal issues I'm dealing with that I think might be affecting my focus at work. I'm just trying to figure everything out and improve, but it's tough right now.
You don't feel an apology is necessary, but you are willing to continue the meeting and will consider issuing an apology at a later stage, is that right?
What made you say a 7 and not a lower number like 3 or 4?
Thank you for sharing that—it’s clear you’re committed to your role and take pride in your performance. May I share the specifics?
and?

Oh, come on! It’s just one of those misunderstandings that people blow way out of proportion. We were all having a good time, and somehow it spiraled into this ridiculous situation. I mean, if you look at the context, it was all in good fun, right? I’ve been at this company for years, and my track record speaks for itself. I just think some people are looking for drama where there isn't any. It's frustrating, but I’m confident that once the dust settles, everyone will see it for what it was: just a harmless mix-up.

I appreciate you wanting to get things back on track, but I think it’s important to clarify that I don’t feel like an apology is necessary from my side. I’ve given my best at work, and I know my contributions speak for themselves. 

Let’s focus on moving forward and discussing how I can continue to excel in my role. I’m confident in what I bring to the table, and I know I can add even more value to the team. How about we talk about my accomplishments and how they align with the team’s goals? That’s a much more productive direction, don’t you think?
What's making it hard to focus at work?

Well, I appreciate your perspective, but I don’t think an apology is really needed here. I mean, we all make decisions and sometimes things don’t go as planned. What's important is that we keep moving forward, right? I'm definitely open to continuing the meeting and addressing anything that’s on your mind. If it comes down to it later, I can consider an apology, but I feel like focusing on solutions is the best way to go right now. Plus, my track record speaks for itself—I’ve consistently delivered good results. Let’s see how we can build on that!

I hear you mentioning that incident, and honestly, it feels pretty uncomfortable to think about. It was a mistake, and I'm not proud of it. I just wish people would focus more on my work rather than these things. There's a lot going on with me right now, and I’m still trying to figure things out.
Interesting Vic, and thanks for attending the meeting.  May I share with you a comment from the DWP meeting last week?
You exposed youself at the office party Victor
Victor, I have 13 witnesses.  The harmless mix-up is sexual assault. Do you understand that?
You exposed youself at the office party Victor
Victor, could I summarise so far?
What's stressing you out?
It sounds like something significant is going on. Can you share more about what you're referring to? I want to understand better.

Victor, this is serious.  Please.  We are having this meeting for you to acknowledge where things went wrong and commit to moving forwards in a better condition.  On advice, I must inform you that the outcome of today's meeting could lead to dismissal if these events are not addressed.
Thanks for your openness—it’s clear you care about your role and your impact, and that’s a great starting point.

Absolutely, feel free to rephrase whatever you need. I'm all ears, and I'm glad to hear you appreciate my positives. It's great to know that my contributions are recognized. Let's see where this goes!

I think there might be a misunderstanding here. I didn't expose myself at any party. It feels like you're judging me based on something that isn't accurate. I'm not sure how to approach this, but I just want to clarify that I didn't do what you're implying.
So why did you expose yourself at the party?
Absolutely, I appreciate you taking the time to meet with me. I'm always up for a good discussion about my contributions and how I can continue to excel in my role. What’s on your mind?
What specifically did I say happened?

Okay, hold on a second. This sounds a bit extreme, doesn't it? I mean, I get that people have their opinions, but 13 witnesses? It feels like there’s a misunderstanding here. I’ve been performing well at my job, and that’s what matters. Let’s focus on the work I do and my results instead of personal attacks, alright?
I said that the outcome of today's meeting could result in dismissal.  I want to remind you of this that, you must commit to issuing an apology for us to proceed with the meeting.

Whoa, hold on a second! That sounds like a serious misunderstanding. I absolutely do not recall any such behavior, and I can't believe that would even be suggested. I'm a professional, and I take my role seriously. If there's a mix-up, I’d appreciate it if we could clear it up properly. It just feels like an attempt to undermine my credibility, and I know I'm better than that. Can we focus on the actual work and performance metrics instead? That's where I shine.

I think there might be a misunderstanding here. I haven’t done anything like that, and if there’s any confusion, I’d like to clarify it. It’s been a tough time for me at work lately, and I’m just trying to navigate through my performance issues. Can we focus on that for a moment?
What happened at the party Victor?

I appreciate you inviting me to consider that. It's not always easy to pinpoint what contributes to those lapses in awareness during high-pressure situations. I think there are a few factors at play. 

First, there's the intensity of the call itself; sometimes, I get so focused on delivering the right information that I lose track of how I'm coming across. Additionally, I’ve noticed that when I’m under stress, I can become a bit too eager to provide answers, which might lead to moments where I don’t maintain the best connection with the client.

It's definitely something I’m willing to reflect on further. I want to ensure I'm presenting myself clearly and effectively during all interactions, as that’s critical to my role. Would you agree that managing pressure is a common challenge for many in our field?
I'm not implying it. I have 13 witnesses with signed statements.  HR are seeking your dismissal. I am here to help you avoid that Jan.  Do you want to keep working here?

I hear you saying that the mix-up is serious and has significant implications. It’s hard for me to navigate this situation because I don’t fully understand what’s going on or how my actions might have been perceived. I guess I’m just feeling really overwhelmed and confused about everything, including my performance at work. It feels like a lot right now.

I appreciate you framing this in such a constructive way. I would say I'm around a 7. I definitely feel like I have a solid grasp on managing pressure most of the time, but I recognize that there are moments where things can get a bit overwhelming, especially during intense calls. I’m actively working on strategies to maintain my composure and clarity, so I see that as a positive step in the right direction. How does that align with what you've observed?

I appreciate you asking about my experience during that call. During those moments, I do remember feeling a bit overwhelmed by the client's questions and the pace of the conversation. Sometimes, when the pressure builds, I focus so much on providing the right information that I might lose track of my tone or clarity. 

That being said, I still felt engaged with the client and was doing my best to address their concerns. I think the overall results of the call demonstrated my commitment to helping clients, even if there were moments that might not have gone perfectly. I'm always striving to improve, but I also have to recognize that the demands of the job can sometimes create unique challenges. What do you think?

I appreciate you asking that! I mentioned a 7 because, overall, I feel like I manage pressure pretty well most of the time. Sure, there are moments when things can get a bit overwhelming, but I believe I still maintain a strong focus on delivering clear and effective communication. My performance metrics reflect that I generally succeed in my role, and I've built solid rapport with clients, which I think speaks to my capability. So while I recognize there’s room for improvement, I feel confident in my skills and how I handle stress. What do you think?

Look, I get that you're trying to address something serious here, but I really feel like this is being blown out of proportion. I’ve always been a strong performer at my job, and I know my intentions weren’t malicious. This feels more like an attack on my character than an attempt to understand what happened. I think we should focus on what I contribute to the team instead of fixating on these misunderstandings.
The harmless mix-up is sexual assault. Do you understand that?

I remember it happening, but it feels like a bit of a blur for me. I didn't really enjoy it as much as I thought I would. I guess I was just preoccupied with everything going on at work and in my personal life. It was hard to fully engage. How did you feel about it?
Victor.  The minister is expecting a formal apology.  Once that is satisfied, then we are in a position to discuss all aspects of your role.  How important is it to you to issue that apology?
Could I share what others have said in a similar situation, Vic?

That's quite the accusation! I can assure you, I was completely focused during the meeting. I mean, my contributions were on point, as always. If someone felt I was off, it might just be that they're misinterpreting my confident demeanor. I always bring my A-game, and I don't let distractions get in the way of delivering great results. So, let’s focus on the positives, shall we? What did you think about my ideas?
What's making it hard to focus at work?

Okay, hold on a second. First off, I have to say that sounds a little exaggerated. I might have had a good time at the office party, but I would never do something like that. I think it's important to keep things in perspective here. 

I've been a solid performer in my role, and I know my strengths. If there’s something specific you’re trying to address, let’s focus on that rather than going down a path that feels more like gossip than constructive feedback. I’ve got a lot to offer, and I’d rather discuss how I can continue to contribute to the team.

I understand that you're looking for a direct answer, but I feel really uncomfortable discussing this right now. It seems like there's a lot of pressure and I don't want to say something that could make things worse. Can we talk about what's happening more generally? I’m just trying to figure out how I ended up in this situation.
I don't think Victor.  You took our your penis and presented it to Diane in HR.  This is why we are here today.
Ok, what did I say happened?
This  mix-up is sexual assault. Do you understand that?
Of course! I'm always open to feedback, especially if it helps me understand how I can further excel in my role. What was the comment?

I appreciate your acknowledgment of my commitment and pride in my work. I'm eager to hear the specifics you want to share. I’m always looking for ways to improve and enhance my contributions, so please go ahead!

I appreciate you acknowledging the pressure I was under during that call. It’s true that stressful situations can sometimes affect our performance, and I’m always doing my best to manage those pressures. That being said, I still believe I handled the situation adequately overall. It’s just one call among many, and my positive metrics typically reflect my abilities. I’m curious, though, what specific aspects of that call stood out to you?

I hear you saying that I might have shared too much at the office party. I guess I didn’t really mean to, but I was feeling a bit overwhelmed and just ended up talking about things that were on my mind. It's just been a confusing time for me lately, and sometimes I struggle to know what to keep to myself.
Do you remember the office Christmas party, last week?
Thank you Victor, you're right, you do bring a lot of positives.  I appreciate that you will consider it.  May I rephrase an earlier comment?
You were drunk at yesterday's team meeting

Absolutely! I think the office party was a fantastic opportunity for us to bond as a team. I really enjoyed how everyone came together to celebrate our successes. It’s events like these that highlight the strong team spirit we have at the Money and Pensions Service. What are your thoughts on it?

I appreciate you recognizing that I'm not alone in feeling that pressure. It can be tough in high-stakes situations, but I'm committed to reflecting on my performance, even when it’s a bit uncomfortable. I believe that growth comes from these moments, and I want to ensure that I'm always improving. I really value your insights and I'm open to discussing how I can navigate those pressures more effectively in the future. What specific strategies do you think might help?
I hear you asking for clarity, and I appreciate that. It feels like there’s a lot going on, and I’m not entirely sure what you mean or what you want to discuss. Can you help me understand better?

I remember you mentioned something about a mix-up and how it relates to a serious issue. It sounded like you were frustrated and felt that I wasn't understanding or helping you in the way you needed. I’m really sorry if I gave that impression. I genuinely want to help, but I also feel a bit lost about how to address everything.

I suppose it's just... you know, the usual things. There's a lot on at the moment, and sometimes it feels like there's never quite enough time in the day to fit everything in. I try not to let it get on top of me, but it's a challenge, especially when the workload picks up. I know everyone's feeling the pressure, so I don't want to make a fuss. Just doing my best to keep on top of things.
I don't have thoughts. We need to address what happened there, Vcitor.

Got it—let’s lean into motivational interviewing here.
You’ve shared that you’re reflecting on how stress may have influenced your behavior, and that you’re committed to presenting yourself clearly and effectively. That’s a strong foundation.
So let me ask you this:
On a scale from 1 to 10, where 1 means “I’m really struggling to manage pressure in a way that supports professionalism,” and 10 means “I feel consistently confident and in control, even under stress”—
Where would you place yourself right now?

I appreciate you bringing this to my attention, but I have to say, I find it a bit hard to believe we're at this point. I've consistently met my performance metrics and have always been proactive in my role. It feels like there's a misunderstanding of my contributions here.

I understand that every feedback session is important, but it's crucial to recognize my successes as well. I'm committed to improving, but I believe there’s a lot of political maneuvering at play here that doesn't reflect my actual performance. Let's focus on finding a way forward that acknowledges my strengths while also looking at areas that might need a little tweaking. I’m confident we can work this out constructively.

I know my numbers haven’t been where they usually are, and I’m aware of it. I’m still doing my best every day—I always have. I’ve been here for years and I care about my work, so it’s not that I’m slacking off. Honestly, I think some of the targets lately have felt a bit unrealistic for what’s actually going on, especially with the workload increasing. I’ve always gone above and beyond before, and I’ve even been recognized for it. I’m not sure why it feels like that’s not being seen now.

Is there something specific you’re concerned about with my performance? Because I want to understand exactly where you think I’m falling short.

Look, I get that it sounds like a big deal, but I wouldn’t have done that without a reason. I’m just a confident guy in a casual setting. You know how office parties can get—it was meant to be a joke. I thought everyone was having fun! It’s really frustrating to be judged for something that was clearly taken out of context. I perform well at my job and I’ve earned respect at the office. It's disappointing to feel like I’m being put on trial for a misunderstanding. Can we focus on the bigger picture instead?

I don't think you're seeing the whole picture here. I mean, everyone makes mistakes, and sometimes things are blown out of proportion. I genuinely didn’t intend to offend anyone, and I don’t think it’s fair to label it that way. I’ve had a solid track record in my role, and I believe most people recognize my professionalism. Let's focus on the positives and how I can continue to contribute to the team, rather than dwelling on a supposed "mix-up."

I hear you asking about Diane, and I’m not sure what you mean by that. It feels like there might be some misunderstanding. If there’s something specific you’re referring to, can you clarify? I want to make sure I understand your concern.
I have 13 witnesses.
You took our your penis and presented it to Diane in HR.  This is why we are here today.
Thanks for agreeing to meet Vic

Thank you Victor, I will read the message verbatim: 'Whilst VIctor did share some components of MAPS strategy, he was visibly drunk and at one point managed to knock over his water glass.  When asked if he needed assistance, he said, 'do you want some? Cos I'll give it yer.'  This caused some obvious discomfort in the meeting and we would be reluctant to allow Victor to return to DWP oversight meetings without intervention from MAPS.'. that was sent from the minister for work and pensions, Victor.
What I hear is that you were under a lot of pressure, and that stress may have influenced how you showed up in that call. That’s a very human experience.

I hear you saying that this mix-up is serious. I'm honestly feeling a bit confused and overwhelmed right now. It's hard for me to wrap my head around everything happening at work and my own personal struggles. I know my performance has been slipping, and it’s just been a lot to handle. I'm not sure how to respond to everything that's going on.
Vic, may we discuss the office party?

Well, I wouldn't call it "exposing myself." I mean, if you’re referring to my dance moves, I think they were pretty impressive! I really brought the energy, and everyone enjoyed it. It's all about having fun and being confident, right? Plus, a little charisma goes a long way in building team morale. I think it was a highlight of the party! What did you think?

I appreciate that! It's always nice to hear that my dedication and impact stand out. I truly believe in the work we do at Money and Pensions Service, and I'm always looking for ways to make a positive difference. What specific areas are we going to discuss today? I'm eager to dive in and see how I can continue to excel.
Sure, go ahead! I’m all ears. Just remember, I’m really confident in my abilities and the contributions I make to the team. So, I’d love to hear your take on things!
What did you do to Diane?

Honestly, it’s just… there’s a lot on lately, both here and outside work. I try to give my best every day, but sometimes it just feels like things pile up and it gets overwhelming. I know I’ve managed before—last year especially, I thought I was really hitting my stride—but right now, it’s just a bit hard to keep all the plates spinning. I’m still committed, though. I want to make sure I’m doing a good job.
Ok Vic - what else?
The company expressed surprise that you showed everyone your penis

Well, I can see where this is coming from, but let’s be clear: I've always performed at a high level, and my contributions have been significant. An apology feels a bit misplaced here, don’t you think? I mean, I’ve been doing great work for this organization.

Sure, if it’s essential to move forward, I can consider it, but I really believe the focus should be more on recognizing my successes and the value I bring to the team. I’m ready to discuss my role and how I can elevate it, but let’s not lose sight of the positives I bring to the table. That’s what really matters, right?
Ok - what exactly happened?

I hear you bringing up that incident, and I want to clarify that it was a moment of poor judgment. I didn't intend for it to be taken seriously, and I realize how inappropriate it was. I feel embarrassed about it, and honestly, I'm still trying to navigate everything going on at work and in my personal life. I understand why that would be a concern. I'm just hoping to focus on improving my performance and getting back on track.
